evidenceItems.quoteOrDescription: Dashboard navigation shows Dashboard, Staff Management, Evaluation Records, Review Reports, and Permission Settings.
Dashboard summary cards show Total Employees, Pending Evaluations, Reviewed This Month, and High Risk Records.
Dashboard filters include Company, Department, and Evaluation Period.
Dashboard shows Export Monthly Summary and notes export permission is unclear.
Employee list page title is 员工列表.
Employee list filters include company, department, group, employee status, and job role.
Employee table columns include employee id, name, company, department, group, job role, status, joined date, supervisor, and latest evaluation date.
Employee row actions include view, edit, and deactivate.
Employee top actions include create employee, bulk import, and export.
employees.csv headers are employee_id, name, company, department, group, role, status, joined_at, and supervisor_id.
employees.csv contains supervisor_id values such as EMP004 and EMP001, indicating a supervisor relationship.
employees.csv includes active and inactive employee status values.
departments.csv headers are department_id, company, name, manager_id, and status.
Some department rows have empty manager_id values.
Evaluation records page title is 评估记录.
Evaluation records filters include period, department, group, status, and score range.
Evaluation records columns include record id, employee name, department, group, period, score, status, submitter, reviewer, submitted time, and reviewed time.
Status labels include draft, submitted, reviewing, approved, and returned.
Evaluation record actions include view detail, edit, submit, review, and export.
evaluation_records.csv headers include record_id, employee_id, evaluation_period, score, status, submitted_by, reviewed_by, submitted_at, and reviewed_at.
evaluation_records.csv includes approved, reviewed, rejected, draft, and submitted status values.
Draft and unreviewed evaluation records have missing score, reviewer, or reviewed_at values.
Evaluation detail sections include employee information, score, performance description, manager review comments, history, and operation logs.
Evaluation detail fields include employee name, department, group, period, score, performance description, risk level, submitter, reviewer, and review comment.
Evaluation detail actions include save draft, submit for review, approve, reject, and view operation log.
Permission settings shows President, Company Admin, Department Manager, Group Leader, Evaluator, and Viewer roles.
Permission columns include View, Create, Edit, Review, Approve, Export, Import, and Configure.
Permission data scopes include all companies, own company, own department, own group, assigned records, and self only.
Business notes describe president all-scope view, company admin company scope, department manager uncertainty, and group leader submission responsibility.
Business notes say the new system should preserve who submitted, reviewed, approved, rejected, or edited a record.
President can view all levels of records, may export reports, and should not necessarily edit every record directly.
Company Admin can manage records in own company, import employee data, and may configure company-level roles.
Department Manager usually reviews own department records; cross-department view and export access are unclear.
Group Leader can submit evaluation records for group members and may edit draft records before submission.
Evaluator can create and submit evaluation records; review or approval permission is unclear.
Viewer can view assigned records and should not create, edit, approve, import, export, or configure permissions.
Workflow notes define draft, submitted, reviewed, approved, and rejected states and actions including submit, review, approve, reject, edit, and export.
Workflow notes list unclear rules for submitted edit, rejected return, approval authority, final approval requirement, and operation log visibility.

claimMappings.claim: The system includes a Dashboard module.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Dashboard appears directly in navigation and has dedicated summary cards and filters.

claimMappings.claim: The system includes a Staff Management module.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Navigation and employee list materials directly support this module.

claimMappings.claim: The system includes an Evaluation Records module.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Navigation, page title, and record table columns directly support this module.

claimMappings.claim: The system includes a Review Reports module.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Review Reports appears in navigation and reporting is described, but there is no dedicated report screen.

claimMappings.claim: The system includes a Permission Settings module.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Navigation and permission settings mock directly support this module.

claimMappings.claim: Employee is a core entity.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Employee page and CSV data directly describe employee records and relationships.

claimMappings.claim: Department is a core entity and data scope.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Department appears in filters, CSV data, and business notes as an access scope.

claimMappings.claim: Group is a likely entity or organizational scope.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Group appears as a filter and CSV field, but there is no standalone group table.

claimMappings.claim: EvaluationRecord is a core entity.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Page columns and CSV data directly describe evaluation records and lifecycle fields.

claimMappings.claim: Role is a core access-control entity.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Permission settings and role notes explicitly list roles.

claimMappings.claim: Permission is a core access-control entity.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Permission settings explicitly show action columns and data scopes.

claimMappings.claim: User or Account is likely needed to model actors and role assignments.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Actors appear in roles and submitted/reviewed fields, but account structure is not shown directly.

claimMappings.claim: AuditLog is likely required for operation history.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Evaluation detail has operation logs and business notes require action traceability.

claimMappings.claim: President has all-scope visibility.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Role notes and business notes directly state all-scope visibility; export/edit still need review.

claimMappings.claim: Company Admin has company-level management responsibility.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Role and business notes support company-level access and import/configure responsibilities.

claimMappings.claim: Department Manager reviews department evaluation records.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Role notes and workflow notes support review responsibility; cross-department visibility is unresolved.

claimMappings.claim: Group Leader submits records for group members.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Role notes directly describe group member submission responsibility.

claimMappings.claim: Evaluator can create and submit evaluation records.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Role notes support create/submit but review/approval authority is unclear.

claimMappings.claim: Viewer is read-only for assigned records.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Role notes explicitly deny mutation permissions.

claimMappings.claim: President can view all evaluation records.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: The role is clearly all-scope, though exact resource-level boundaries should still be implemented explicitly.

claimMappings.claim: President may export review reports.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Role notes say export may be allowed and dashboard exposes export; this is still a review item.

claimMappings.claim: Company Admin can import employee data for their company.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Role notes mention import and employee list has import action.

claimMappings.claim: Department Manager can view department-scoped evaluation records.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Department scope is supported, but cross-department access is unresolved.

claimMappings.claim: Department Manager can review evaluation records.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Role notes and workflow notes support review, but final approval is separate and unclear.

claimMappings.claim: Department Manager export permission is unclear.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Export action exists and role notes call out uncertainty.

claimMappings.claim: Group Leader can create evaluation records for group members.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Role notes support group member record creation and workflow creation.

claimMappings.claim: Group Leader may edit draft records before submission.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Draft edit is supported, but submitted edit behavior is unresolved.

claimMappings.claim: Evaluator can create evaluation records.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Role notes directly say Evaluator can create records.

claimMappings.claim: Viewer can view assigned records only.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Viewer notes and assigned-record scope support this permission.

claimMappings.claim: Evaluation review workflow runs draft -> submitted -> reviewed -> approved, with rejected as an alternate outcome.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: States are strongly supported, but approval, edit, and rejection rules remain incomplete.

claimMappings.claim: Department Manager scope must be enforced.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Scope enforcement follows from role notes, but cross-department access must be confirmed.

claimMappings.claim: Export permission must be explicit.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Export is visible in multiple places and is high risk.

claimMappings.claim: Draft records can be submitted and reviewed.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Submit and review are supported, but surrounding edit/final-state rules need review.

claimMappings.claim: Evaluation records must preserve lifecycle fields.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: CSV and page columns define lifecycle data, including nullable values.

claimMappings.claim: Department Manager cross-department visibility needs confirmation.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: The source materials explicitly leave this unresolved, so it remains an open question rather than a fact.

claimMappings.claim: Group Leader edit-after-submit behavior needs confirmation.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Workflow notes ask whether submitted records can be edited before review starts.

claimMappings.claim: Final approval authority needs confirmation.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Approval action exists, but no source confirms the final approver role.

claimMappings.claim: Export permission needs confirmation.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Export actions exist, but role-level export permission is not settled.

claimMappings.claim: Final-state approval requirement needs confirmation.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Reviewed, approved, and final states must not be collapsed without business confirmation.

claimMappings.claim: Rejected-record return path needs confirmation.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: The workflow notes say records may be rejected and returned, but not which state they return to.

claimMappings.claim: Operation log visibility needs confirmation.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Operation logs appear, but visibility by role is unresolved.

claimMappings.claim: Employee deletion or deactivation behavior needs confirmation.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Only deactivate appears in the UI, so permanent deletion must not be assumed.

claimMappings.claim: Employee records should be deactivated rather than permanently deleted.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: This is a reasonable design assumption but still requires review.

claimMappings.claim: Risk level may be derived from score or reviewer judgment.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Risk level is visible but derivation is not confirmed.

claimMappings.claim: Operation logs are required for auditability.
claimMappings.reasoningSummary: Auditability is supported, but exact log model and visibility still need review.
